spec_match.rationale: Uses `calc(base + scale * ratio)` with length/length producing a unitless number — the canonical spec demonstration.

interactive.rationale: Multiple sliders driving a live readout of the resulting size and the calc expression.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises sliders driving the headline; the page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Doesn't show invalid combinations (length + angle), the error fallback when types don't unify, nor the precision behaviour at extreme inputs.

openQuestions.title: Show the invalid-combinations error
openQuestions.detail: Add a slider that intentionally produces an invalid combination (e.g. add an angle to a length) and surface the CSS error / fallback. Visitors should see what 'typed' actually means.

openQuestions.title: Compare to the pre-v140 hack
openQuestions.detail: The em-to-unitless concept mentions `tan(atan2(...))` as the old trick. Reference and demonstrate the contrast briefly here so authors know why the new syntax matters.

openQuestions.title: Show in different unit families
openQuestions.detail: Sliders are pixels here; demonstrate em/em, %/%, time/time as well so visitors learn the breadth of the spec.

summary: Clean canonical demo of the spec change. Strengthen with an invalid-combination probe and a before/after vs the old tan(atan2) workaround.
